title: Novartis — Contacts
html:
Novartis Contact Map & Relationship Intelligence

Governing Thought

The Novartis contact map is the least-documented of any active ZoomRx account of this tenure — 7 years and 24 projects, yet no individual contact names are available in SharePoint project data. This is both a data gap and a structural observation: the relationship has been sustained through multiple brand-team engagements rather than a single champion or centralized buyer. The procurement file-sharing activity (Jan 2026) is the strongest signal that a centralized buying function exists and is evaluating ZoomRx alongside other vendors. Contact identification — especially procurement contacts, above-brand connectors, and brand-level decision-makers on the three untouched mega-brands (Entresto, Cosentyx, Kesimpta) — is the single most important action item for this account.

Novartis Commercial Organization (Inferred Structure)

Novartis US Commercial / Medical Affairs
  |
  |--- Oncology
  |      |--- Breast Cancer (Kisqali) --- [Contact TBD]
  |      |--- Prostate Cancer / GU (Pluvicto, mHSPC) --- [Contact TBD]
  |      |--- Hematology/Oncology (Scemblix/CML) --- [Contact TBD]
  |
  |--- Hematology
  |      |--- PNH (Fabhalta/Iptacopan) --- [Contact TBD]
  |      |--- ITP (Promacta) --- [Contact TBD]
  |
  |--- Cardiovascular
  |      |--- Heart Failure (Entresto) --- [Contact TBD]
  |      |--- Lipids/CV (Leqvio) --- [Contact TBD]
  |
  |--- Immunology / Dermatology
  |      |--- CSU (Remibrutinib/Rhapsido) --- [Contact TBD]
  |      |--- Psoriasis/PsA/AS (Cosentyx) --- [Contact TBD]
  |
  |--- Neuroscience
  |      |--- Multiple Sclerosis (Kesimpta) --- [Contact TBD]
  |
  |--- Rare Disease / Gene Therapy
  |      |--- SMA (Zolgensma) --- [Contact TBD]
  |
  |--- Pipeline / Clinical
  |      |--- Anticoagulation (Abelacimab) --- [Contact TBD]
  |      |--- CV Pipeline (Pelacarsen) --- [Contact TBD]
  |
  |--- Cross-Functional
         |--- Procurement --- [Contact TBD — active vendor evaluation detected]
         |--- SAGAN / Analytics / AI --- [Contact TBD — demo initiated]
         |--- Medical Affairs --- [Contact TBD]

Note: This org chart is entirely reconstructed from project data and SharePoint folder names. No individual contacts are available. [CRITICAL: Populate from Kimble SOWs, email correspondence, and account team debriefs.]

Tier 1: Established Contacts (Active Business)

Status: No Named Contacts Available

Despite 7 years and 24 projects, no individual contact names have been identified in the available SharePoint data. This is the account's most significant relationship intelligence gap.

Inferred contacts by project activity:

Project(s) Therapeutic Area Likely Contact Role Priority to Identify
Rhapsido PET (Wave 5, active) Immunology/Derm (CSU) CSU Brand Insights/Analytics Lead HIGHEST — this is the recurring revenue anchor
Abelacimab HCP-Pt + MI (active) Anticoagulation Pipeline/Clinical Marketing Lead HIGH — currently active project
PSP Study Proposal (active, Apr 2026) Unknown Insights or Medical Affairs Lead HIGH — active proposal
SAGAN Answers demo (active) Cross-functional Analytics/AI Team Lead HIGH — strategic integration signal
Procurement file sharing (Jan 2026) Procurement Vendor Management/Procurement Lead HIGH — centralized evaluation underway

Tier 2: Historical Contacts (Past Projects)

These brand teams have engaged ZoomRx for completed projects. The contacts are warm but not currently active.

Brand Project(s) Completed Last Activity Likely Contact Role Reactivation Priority
Kisqali HCP-Pt (2024) 2024 Breast Cancer Insights Lead HIGH — PET upsell candidate
Pluvicto HCP-Pt (2024, 2025), PET (2025) Mar 2025 GU Oncology Insights Lead HIGH — MSL/MEE and continued PET
Scemblix Zero-cost PET (2023), HCP-Pt (2023) Oct 2023 CML Brand Team Lead MEDIUM — paid PET conversion
Fabhalta/PNH HCP-Pt (2024) Jan 2024 Hematology Insights Lead HIGH — PET upsell candidate
Leqvio HCP-Pt (2023) Oct 2023 CV Commercial Lead MEDIUM — PET/ATU candidate
Pelacarsen HCP-Pt (2023) Oct 2023 CV Pipeline Lead MEDIUM — launch tracking if approved
Remibrutinib CSU HCP-Pt (2025) Mar 2025 CSU Insights Lead (likely same as PET) Active via PET
mHSPC HCP-Pt (2025) Apr 2025 GU Oncology Lead MEDIUM
Zolgensma Caregiver + HCP Qual (2023) 2023 Rare Disease/Gene Therapy Lead LOW — gene therapy is niche
ITP/wAIHA/PNH Multi-indication HCP-Pt (2023) Nov 2023 Hematology Franchise Lead MEDIUM

Tier 3: Untouched Decision Makers (No ZoomRx History)

These brand teams have never engaged ZoomRx. They represent the largest untapped contact development opportunity.

Brand Revenue Scale Current Vendor(s) Path to Relationship Priority
Entresto ~$7.3B [UNVERIFIED] Unknown Cross-TA referral from existing Novartis contacts; Entresto is large enough to have a dedicated insights team HIGHEST — largest untapped brand
Cosentyx ~$4.5B [UNVERIFIED] Unknown May share contacts with Remibrutinib/CSU team (immunology overlap) HIGH — biosimilar defense tracking opportunity
Kesimpta Growing MS brand Unknown Neuroscience team is separate; requires new contact development MEDIUM — no existing Novartis neuro contacts

Procurement Intelligence

What We Know

The SharePoint folder "2024-2025 Project Files to be shared with the Procurement Team" (Jan 2026) contains deliverables from:

  • PNH Wave 1
  • Kisqali HCP-Pt
  • Pluvicto HCP-Pt
  • Remibrutinib DT
  • Remibrutinib PET

Interpretation: Novartis procurement is reviewing ZoomRx deliverables across therapeutic areas — this is vendor evaluation behavior. The breadth of deliverables shared (5 different projects across 4 brands) suggests a portfolio-level assessment, not a single-brand review.

Implications:

  1. A centralized procurement contact exists and is actively evaluating ZoomRx
  2. The evaluation spans multiple TAs, suggesting an enterprise-level vendor management process
  3. ZoomRx's cross-TA deliverable quality is being assessed holistically
  4. This may lead to a framework agreement (positive) or competitive RFP process (neutral-to-negative)

Action items:

  • Identify the procurement contact(s) — ask the Rhapsido PET and Abelacimab project leads
  • Prepare a portfolio-level case study document for procurement evaluation
  • Proactively propose a framework agreement before being pulled into a competitive RFP

SAGAN Analytics Contact

What We Know

The "SAGAN Answers demo" folder contains Rhapsido PET Wave 5 Full Report and Remibrutinib raw data. SAGAN is Novartis's internal AI/analytics platform.

Interpretation: Someone at Novartis is exploring whether ZoomRx data can be ingested into SAGAN for internal analytics. This person is likely in an analytics, AI, or data science function — not a traditional brand insights role.

Action items:

  • Identify who initiated the SAGAN demo — this is a potential above-brand champion
  • Clarify whether the demo was Novartis-initiated (pull) or ZoomRx-initiated (push)
  • Understand SAGAN's capabilities and how ZoomRx data would be used within it
  • Position ZoomRx as an insights partner in the SAGAN ecosystem, not just a data feed

Relationship Intelligence

Working Style Do's and Don'ts

DO:

  • Lead with cross-TA versatility — Novartis values vendors who understand multiple therapeutic areas; ZoomRx's 6-TA track record is the primary differentiator
  • Use completed HCP-Pt studies as brand-specific proof points — each completed study is a warm lead for the same brand team
  • Engage procurement proactively — the vendor evaluation is happening; being proactive positions ZoomRx favorably vs. waiting to be evaluated
  • Explore the SAGAN connection — the AI integration signal is strategically important and could create a new type of above-brand relationship
  • Present longitudinal value — Rhapsido Wave 5 data demonstrates the compounding value of tracking over time; use this to pitch PET on other brands

DON'T:

  • Don't assume brand teams coordinate — each TA likely has independent insights budgets and decision-makers; what works on Rhapsido may not transfer to Kisqali without separate relationship building
  • Don't neglect the procurement signal — ignoring the vendor evaluation and continuing brand-by-brand selling could result in being surprised by a competitive RFP
  • Don't let SAGAN define ZoomRx as a data utility — if the integration positions ZoomRx as a raw data supplier, analytical value and margins compress
  • Don't rely on Rhapsido PET alone — it is the revenue anchor but also the single point of failure

Meeting Cadences

Cadence Contacts
Regular project meetings [Populate — Rhapsido PET, Abelacimab HCP-Pt project leads]
Quarterly or ad hoc [Populate — procurement, SAGAN team]
Reactivation outreach needed Kisqali, Pluvicto, Fabhalta, Leqvio brand teams

[Populate all cadences from account team debrief.]

Contact Map Gaps (Action Items)

The following information is missing and should be populated as highest priority:

Critical (Immediate)

  • Name and contact for Rhapsido PET Wave 5 brand-side lead (this is the recurring revenue anchor — must have contact intelligence)
  • Name and contact for Abelacimab HCP-Pt project lead (active project)
  • Name and contact for PSP Study Proposal lead (active proposal)
  • Name and contact for Novartis procurement team evaluating vendors
  • Name and contact for SAGAN demo initiator

High Priority (Within 30 Days)

  • Kisqali brand insights lead (highest-value PET upsell)
  • Pluvicto brand insights lead (MSL/MEE and PET continuation)
  • Fabhalta brand insights lead (PET upsell)
  • Entresto insights team identification (largest untapped brand)

Medium Priority (Within 90 Days)

  • Cosentyx brand team contact (biosimilar defense)
  • Kesimpta brand team contact (MS growth brand)
  • Leqvio/CV franchise contact (PET/ATU)
  • Novartis Medical Affairs leadership (MSL/MEE pathway)

[Populate from Kimble SOW data, project email threads, LinkedIn research, and account team debriefs.]

Back to Accounts Hub | Home
